title: Skill Discovery, Trigger Descriptions, and Boundaries · Trigger Forensics
summary: Make explicit and implicit invocation reliable without false positives. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a trigger evaluation dataset and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Front-load use cases and trigger terms in a concise skill description.
Write diverse prompts that should activate the skill.
Write adjacent prompts that must not activate the skill.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: agent ext triggers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Description Triggers trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for front-load use cases and trigger terms in a concise skill description.

content.text: For Description Triggers, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Description Triggers, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-trigger-evals-d6qug3: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Trigger Evals into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-trigger-evals-d6qug3:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Trigger Evals applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure missed triggers, false triggers, and competing-skill selection.
